speaker: Unknown
en: You are a product manager shipping product without knowing how to write code, barely knowing how to review code.
zh: 你是一个产品经理，发布产品却不知道如何写代码，几乎不知道如何审查代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have zero technical background, did music in high school ... when Sonnet 3.5 came out.
zh: 我完全没有技术背景，在高中是学音乐的……当 Sonnet 3.5 出来的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember watching a YouTube video building apps using Bolt or Lovable.
zh: 我记得看过一个 YouTube 视频使用 Bolt 或 Lovable 构建应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: It basically felt like someone came up to me and said, "You have superpowers now."
zh: 感觉就像有人走过来对我说：“你现在有超能力了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: These days, you're using Cursor with Claude Code.
zh: 这些天，你在使用 Cursor 和 Claude Code。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're non-technical like me, code is terrifying, but AI just makes it so much possible.
zh: 如果你像我一样是非技术人员，代码是很可怕的，但 AI 使得很多事情变得如此可能。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the next coming years, I think everyone's going to become a builder.
zh: 在未来几年，我认为每个人都会成为构建者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Titles are going to collapse and responsibilities are going to collapse.
zh: 头衔将会崩塌，职责将会崩塌。

speaker: Unknown
en: The main challenge people have is reviewing the code that AI has written.
zh: 人们面临的主要挑战是审查 AI 写的代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very difficult for me to catch mistakes.
zh: 对我来说，发现错误是非常困难的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I'll do is basically /review.
zh: 我要做的基本上就是 /review。

speaker: Unknown
en: This tells Claude to start reviewing its own code, but what's even cooler is I have Codex as well as Cursor open.
zh: 这告诉 Claude 开始审查它自己的代码，但更酷的是我也打开了 Codex 和 Cursor。

speaker: Unknown
en: I will have each of them review the code.
zh: 我会让它们每一个都审查代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: This comes back to this quote.
zh: 这回到了那句引言。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think everyone's always hearing.
zh: 我想大家总是听到。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not that you will be replaced by AI.
zh: 不是说你将被 AI 取代。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'll be replaced by someone who's better at using AI than you.
zh: 你将被一个比你更擅长使用 AI 的人取代。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the best time to be a junior, contrary to what a lot of people are saying, how there's no more junior roles out there.
zh: 这是成为初级人员的最佳时机，尽管很多人说外面已经没有初级职位了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's true, but also when else in history could you get out of school and just build a startup on your own?
zh: 是的，这是真的，但在历史上还有什么时候你能刚走出学校就独自构建一家初创公司？

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Zevi Arnovitz.
zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是 Zevi Arnovitz。

speaker: Unknown
en: Zevi is a PM at Meta.
zh: Zevi 是 Meta 的一名 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: Prior to that, he was a PM at Wix, and this is a truly remarkable conversation that every non-technical product person needs to hear.
zh: 在那之前，他是 Wix 的一名 PM，这是一个真正非凡的对话，每个非技术产品人员都需要听听。

speaker: Unknown
en: Zevi is super young and has no technical background, but as a smart, young, ambitious person, has learned how to use Cursor and Claude Code to build significant and real products completely on his own, and he's created his own very clever and effective workflow that everyone listening can copy.
zh: Zevi 非常年轻且没有技术背景，但作为一个聪明、年轻、有抱负的人，他学会了如何使用 Cursor 和 Claude Code 完全独自构建重要且真实的产品，并且他创建了自己非常聪明且有效的工作流，每个听众都可以复制。

speaker: Unknown
en: To make that copying even easier, at the top of the show notes of this episode, you can download all of the prompts and /commands and start doing all of this yourself.
zh: 为了让这种复制更容易，在本期节目的说明顶部，你可以下载所有的提示词和 /commands 并开始自己做所有这些事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Zevi shows you how to work with cursor to quickly add your ideas to Linear to explore your idea with AI, how to develop your plan, how to then build the thing, and then have different LLMs review your code and update your documentation, and then use all of this as a learning opportunity to develop your own sense of how things work.
zh: Zevi 向你展示如何使用 cursor 将你的想法快速添加到 Linear 中，用 AI 探索你的想法，如何制定你的计划，如何构建那个东西，然后让不同的 LLM 审查你的代码并更新你的文档，然后利用所有这些作为学习机会，培养你自己对事物运作方式的感知。

speaker: Unknown
en: I haven't stopped thinking about this conversation since we had it, and everyone needs to pay attention to what AI is unlocking for non-technical people.
zh: 自从我们那次谈话以来，我就一直在思考这次对话，每个人都需要关注 AI 正在为非技术人员解锁什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: A huge thank you to Tal Raviv for encouraging me to meet Zevi.
zh: 非常感谢 Tal Raviv 鼓励我去见 Zevi。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you enjoy this podcast, don't forget to subscribe and follow it in your favorite podcasting app or YouTube.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个播客，别忘了在你最喜欢的播客应用或 YouTube 上订阅和关注它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It helps tremendously.
zh: 这会有巨大的帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you become an annual subscriber of my newsletter, you get 19 premium products for free for an entire year including Lovable, Replit, Bolt, Gamma, n8n, Linear, Devin, PostHog, Superhuman, Descript, Wispr Flow, Perplexity, Warp, Granola, Magic Patterns, Raycast, ChatPRD, Mobbin, and Stripe Atlas.
zh: 如果你成为我新闻通讯的年度订阅者，你将免费获得 19 个高级产品整整一年，包括 Lovable、Replit、Bolt、Gamma、n8n、Linear、Devin、PostHog、Superhuman、Descript、Wispr Flow、Perplexity、Warp、Granola、Magic Patterns、Raycast、ChatPRD、Mobbin 和 Stripe Atlas。

speaker: Unknown
en: Head on over to lennysnewsletter.com and click product pass.
zh: 前往 lennysnewsletter.com 并点击 product pass。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Zevi Arnovitz after a short word from our sponsors.
zh: 话虽如此，在简短赞助商广告之后，我为你带来 Zevi Arnovitz。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by 10Web, the company that pioneered AI website building before ChatGPT.
zh: 本期节目由 10Web 为您带来，该公司在 ChatGPT 之前开创了 AI 网站构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the last three years, over two million websites have been generated with 10Web's vibe coding platform.
zh: 在过去三年中，超过 200 万个网站通过 10Web 的 vibe coding 平台生成。

speaker: Unknown
en: 10Web's vibe coding platform is a powerful way to build websites.
zh: 10Web 的 vibe coding 平台是构建网站的一种强大方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think of it as lovable for WordPress, front end and backend.
zh: 把它想象成 WordPress 版的 lovable，前端和后端。

speaker: Unknown
en: Users can build any website at any complexity, e-commerce, portfolios, information websites, blogs, and it comes with the WordPress admin panel and thousands of ready to use plugins.
zh: 用户可以构建任何复杂程度的任何网站，电商、作品集、信息网站、博客，它配有 WordPress 管理面板和数千个即用型插件。

speaker: Unknown
en: 10Web also offers website generation as an API as a service for SaaS companies, marketplaces, hosting providers, MSPs and agencies.
zh: 10Web 还提供作为 API 服务的网站生成，面向 SaaS 公司、市场、托管提供商、MSP 和代理商。

speaker: Unknown
en: SaaS companies can embed it via API so that users can launch AI generated sites directly inside of their platform, connected to their own data.
zh: SaaS 公司可以通过 API 嵌入它，以便用户可以直接在其平台内部启动 AI 生成的网站，连接到他们自己的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: Agencies and MSPs can get a white label dashboard to manage clients and resell under their brand.
zh: 代理商和 MSP 可以获得一个白标仪表板来管理客户并以自己的品牌转售。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hosting providers can self-host the API builder on their own infrastructure.
zh: 托管提供商可以在自己的基础设施上自托管 API 构建器。

speaker: Unknown
en: Check it out at 10web.io/lenny and use code Lenny for exclusive free credits and 30% off API or white labeled solutions.
zh: 访问 10web.io/lenny 并使用代码 Lenny 获得独家免费积分以及 API 或白标解决方案 30% 的折扣。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the number 10web.io/lenny, vibe coding platform as an API.
zh: 那是 10web.io/lenny，作为 API 的 vibe coding 平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today's episode is brought to you by DX, the developer intelligence platform designed by leading researchers.
zh: 今天的节目由 DX 为您带来，DX 是由领先研究人员设计的开发者智能平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: To thrive in the AI era, organizations need to adapt quickly, but many organization leaders struggle to answer pressing questions like, which tools are working?
zh: 为了在 AI 时代蓬勃发展，组织需要快速适应，但许多组织领导者难以回答紧迫的问题，比如，哪些工具正在起作用？

speaker: Unknown
en: How are they being used?
zh: 它们是如何被使用的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's actually driving value?
zh: 到底是什么在驱动价值？

speaker: Unknown
en: DX provides the data and insights that leaders need to navigate this shift.
zh: DX 提供领导者所需的数据和洞察力来应对这一转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: With DX, companies like Dropbox, Booking.com, Adyen, and Intercom get a deep understanding of how AI is providing value to their developers and what impact AI is having on engineering productivity.
zh: 有了 DX，像 Dropbox、Booking.com、Adyen 和 Intercom 这样的公司可以深入了解 AI 如何为他们的开发者提供价值，以及 AI 对工程生产力有什么影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: To learn more, visit DX's website at getdx.com/lenny, that's getdx.com/lenny.
zh: 要了解更多，请访问 DX 的网站 getdx.com/lenny，也就是 getdx.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Zevi, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Zevi，非常感谢你来到这里，欢迎来到播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thanks for having me, Lenny.
zh: 谢谢你的邀请，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm a huge fan of the show and tons of people that I've admired most and learned the most from.
zh: 我是这个节目的超级粉丝，这里有我最钦佩和学到最多的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been on here, so it's a crazy moment for me.
zh: 我也是这里的嘉宾，所以对我来说这是一个疯狂的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm really excited for this.
zh: 我真的很期待这一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really appreciate that.
zh: 我真的很感激这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to start by reading actually a note I got about you from Tal Raviv, who is a previous podcast guest, many times newsletter collaborator.
zh: 我想先读一下我收到的一条关于你的留言，来自 Tal Raviv，他是之前的播客嘉宾，也是多次通讯稿的协作者。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the most AI forward product managers that I know I've learned a ton from him.
zh: 他是我认识的最具 AI 超前思维的产品经理之一，我从他那里学到了很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So here's what he said about you when he introduced us.
zh: 这是他在介绍我们时关于你的说法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Zevi is the most hands-on vibe coding PM I know, and I've personally learned so much from him.
zh: Zevi 是我知道的最亲自动手的 vibe 编程产品经理，我个人从他那里学到了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: His engineers at Meta ask him to teach them how to do what he does.
zh: 他在 Meta 的工程师让他教他们如何做他做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every time we get coffee, I repeatedly get this feeling of everyone needs to be hearing this.
zh: 每次我们一起喝咖啡，我总是会有这种感觉，每个人都应该听听这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's so nice.
zh: 这太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's the goal.
zh: 这就是目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the goal of this conversation is to help more people hear what you figured out.
zh: 这就是我们对话的目标，帮助更多人听到你弄明白的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to get very hands-on.
zh: 我们将非常亲自动手。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to do a lot of show versus tell, showing people what you've figured out about how to be a PM, a non-technical PM building stuff.
zh: 我们将做很多展示而不仅仅是讲述，向人们展示你关于如何做产品经理、非技术产品经理构建东西所弄明白的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to give people a little bit of background on you because I think this is going to inspire a lot of listeners to feel like they can also do what we're about to show you.
zh: 我想给人们一些关于你的背景介绍，因为我认为这将启发许多听众，让他们觉得他们也可以做我们即将向你展示的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is going to look very advanced, but just give people a little bit of sense of just your background.
zh: 这看起来会很高级，但只是给人们一点关于你背景的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm very non-technical.
zh: 我非常非技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have zero technical background.
zh: 我零技术背景。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did music in high school.
zh: 高中学的是音乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of Israelis do technology units in the Army.
zh: 很多以色列人在军队的技术单位服役。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was not in a tech unit.
zh: 我不在技术单位。

speaker: Unknown
en: And basically a year ago, I was traveling with my wife for three months in Asia and we were in Japan and that was around when Sonnet 3.5 came out.
zh: 基本上一年前，我和妻子在亚洲旅行了三个月，我们在日本，那时 Sonnet 3.5 刚出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember watching a YouTube video.
zh: 我记得看了一个 YouTube 视频。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it was either Greg Isenberg or Riley Brown and they were basically building apps using, it was either Bolt or Lovable, just using AI.
zh: 我想是 Greg Isenberg 或者 Riley Brown，他们基本上是在使用 AI 构建应用，用的是 Bolt 或者 Lovable，只是用 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was like a crazy moment for me because I was watching this and it basically felt like someone came up to me and said, "Hey Zevi, there's this cool new technology you should check out.
zh: 对我来说这是一个疯狂的时刻，因为我在看这个视频时，基本上感觉就像有人走上前对我说，“嘿 Zevi，有这个很酷的新技术你应该看看。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should really give it a try.
zh: 你真的应该试试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, and by the way, you have superpowers now."
zh: 哦，顺便说一下，你现在有超能力了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the second I got home from Japan, I didn't even unpack my bags, ran to my computer, opened Bolt, opened an account, and for the past year I've been building.
zh: 我从日本回到家后，我甚至没打开行李，就跑到我的电脑前，打开 Bolt，开了个账户，在过去的一年里我一直在构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the last thing I'll say on that is we talked about this a bit before we started recording, but I was prepping with Claude for the episode and I was trying to clarify what my goal is for this episode.
zh: 关于这一点我想说的最后一件事是，我们在开始录音前稍微聊过一点，但我当时正和 Claude 一起准备这一集，我试图澄清我这一集的目标是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Claude said, "If people walk away thinking how amazing you are, you failed.
zh: Claude 说，“如果人们离开时觉得你多么了不起，你就失败了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if people walk away and open their computer and start building, you've succeeded."
zh: 如果人们离开时打开他们的电脑并开始构建，你就成功了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I really hope that we can inspire some people to do the same.
zh: 所以我真的很希望我们能启发一些人做同样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that so much.
zh: 我太喜欢这个了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like that should be the goal for my podcast.
zh: 我觉得这应该是我播客的目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're like, "I love that guest."
zh: 如果你想，“我喜欢那个嘉宾。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's less of a win.
zh: 这不算什么胜利。

speaker: Unknown
en: If it's just like, "Oh, I'm so inspired to do the thing that they figured out, that is the real win."
zh: 如果只是像，“噢，我很受启发去做他们弄明白的事情，那才是真正的胜利。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I love, Claude is the best.
zh: 我喜欢，Claude 是最好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I agree.
zh: 我同意。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's dive in and give people, let's start with kind of a high level overview of how you operate and you use AI in your job.
zh: 那么让我们深入探讨，给人们，让我们先从高层次概述一下你是如何运作以及在工作中使用 AI 的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the core tools and just what's kind of like the frame of reference for the workflow that you figured out and how you operate?
zh: 核心工具有哪些，你弄明白的工作流的参考框架大致是什么，以及你是如何运作的？

speaker: Unknown
en: This all started where I was a project's power user.
zh: 这一切都始于我是一个项目的重度用户。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love projects, GPT projects.
zh: 我喜欢项目，GPT 项目。

speaker: Lenny
en: ChatGPT projects?
zh: ChatGPT 项目？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, exactly.
zh: 是的，没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: GPT projects and Claude projects, which are basically a shared folder of chats which share both custom instructions and shared knowledge base.
zh: GPT 项目和 Claude 项目，基本上是一个聊天的共享文件夹，它们共享自定义指令和共享知识库。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it was around when GPT started using memory where I thought it was interesting, but it really annoyed me because I do a bunch of different things.
zh: 我认为大概是在 GPT 开始使用记忆功能的时候，我觉得很有趣，但这真的很烦我，因为我做很多不同的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm a terrible runner, I'm a PM, I was a student, psychology student, so I had all these different facets of life.
zh: 我是一个很差的跑者，我是一个 PM，我曾经是一个学生，心理学学生，所以我生活中的这些不同的方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what happened was the memory feature was mixing stuff up.
zh: 发生的情况是，记忆功能把东西搞混了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So like I talked to GPT about running and it would say, "Oh yeah, after this 5K, you're going to crush all your next product reviews."
zh: 所以就像我和 GPT 谈论跑步，它会说，“哦是的，在这场 5K 比赛之后，你将搞定你所有的下一个产品审查。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, okay, I understand that you have that in your memory, but it's just not relevant.
zh: 就像是，好吧，我知道你那个在你的记忆里，但这只是不相关。

speaker: Unknown
en: And projects basically allows you to compartmentalize and have things within the right context.
zh: 项目基本上允许你划分区间，并把东西放在正确的上下文里。

speaker: Unknown
en: So tracking back to the story I told when we came back from Japan, I started building this app.
zh: 所以追溯回我当我们从日本回来时讲的故事，我开始构建这个应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first thing I noticed was that these products were built in a way where, and when I say these products, I mean Bolt and Lovable, were built in a way where they were super eager to write code.
zh: 我注意到的第一件事是，这些产品的构建方式是，当我说这些产品时，我是指 Bolt 和 Lovable，它们的构建方式是它们超级热衷于写代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: So their system prompt was you're a coding agent.
zh: 所以它们的系统提示是，你是一个编码智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when you'd write something, they'd straight away start coding.
zh: 所以当你写东西时，它们会直接开始编码。

speaker: Unknown
en: So at the beginning of a project, this was super fun and exciting because they just go and start building your app.
zh: 所以在项目开始时，这超级有趣和令人兴奋，因为它们直接去开始构建你的应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: But later on when things got more complex, this created much more problems because planning is really important when you're implementing something technical and let's say you're implementing payments or something that's going to be a change to your database.
zh: 但后来当事情变得更复杂时，这产生了更多问题，因为当你实现技术性的东西时规划真的很重要，比如说你在实现支付或者将要改变数据库的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: If the coding agent is just like, "All right, I got it."
zh: 如果编码智能体只是像，“好的，我明白了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And just starts writing code, this always results in terrible things, some really gnarly bugs that I had.
zh: 然后就开始写代码，这总是导致糟糕的事情，我遇到了一些非常棘手的错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: And to mitigate this, what I did was I created sort of a CTO.
zh: 为了缓解这种情况，我做的是创建了一个 CTO。

speaker: Unknown
en: So again, I'm not technical.
zh: 所以再说一次，我不懂技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have been in product for a while, but I know zero stuff about code.
zh: 我做产品已经有一段时间了，但我对代码一无所知。

speaker: Unknown
en: So basically what I did was I created a CTO with the custom prompt of it being the complete technical owner of the project.
zh: 所以基本上我所做的是用自定义提示创建了一个 CTO，作为项目的完整技术负责人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I told it, "I own the problem.

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that first making your code base AI native is a really important step, and I think this needs to be done by technical people.
zh: 我认为首先让你的代码库 AI 化是非常重要的一步，我认为这需要由技术人员来完成。

speaker: Unknown
en: So basically my codebase has a ton of just plain text in it.
zh: 所以基本上我的代码库里有大量的纯文本。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it will have a bunch of markdown files that explain to agents how to work in certain areas of the code base and high level structure so that the agents navigate through the code base easier.
zh: 所以它将有一堆 markdown 文件，向智能体解释如何在代码库的某些领域工作以及高级结构，以便智能体更容易地浏览代码库。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that if this is set up in a really good way, I still don't think PMs should be shipping heavy database chain migrations or any big project, but contained UI projects, especially if you just build it, create the PR and send it to a dev to do the final finishes.
zh: 我认为如果这设置得非常好的话，我仍然不认为 PM（产品经理）应该去进行繁重的数据库链迁移或任何大项目，但受限的 UI 项目，尤其是如果你只是构建它、创建 PR 并将其发给开发人员做最后的收尾工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's definitely something that's possible.
zh: 我认为这绝对是可能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we're going to see that a lot in the next coming years.
zh: 我认为我们在接下来的几年里会经常看到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think basically everyone's going to become a builder, so it should be really interesting.
zh: 我认为基本上每个人都会成为构建者，所以这应该非常有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So your advice here is as a PM, maybe don't go right to Cursor, start building, shipping, trying to ship features to production, especially complicated features.
zh: 所以你这里的建议是，作为 PM，也许不要直接去 Cursor，开始构建、发布、尝试将功能发布到生产环境，尤其是复杂的功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think we'll get there?
zh: 你认为我们会达到那一步吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think in a couple years, PMs will be doing this and it'll feel less scary and crazy?
zh: 你认为在几年后，PM 会做这些事情并且感觉不那么可怕和疯狂吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: If there are PMs.
zh: 如果还有 PM 的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think titles are going to collapse and responsibilities are going to collapse and everyone's just going to be building.
zh: 是的，我认为头衔将会崩塌，责任将会崩塌，每个人都将只是在构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: I definitely think that the models, the context window is getting bigger, the models are getting smarter and I definitely see how PMs or any other background can be writing.
zh: 我绝对认为这些模型、上下文窗口正在变大，模型正变得更聪明，我绝对可以看到 PM 或任何其他背景的人是如何在写代码的。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the moment, I wouldn't wait for that.
zh: 目前，我不会等待那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would use this as a collaborative learning opportunity to work with your dev team.
zh: 我会将其作为一个协作学习的机会，与你的开发团队一起工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be difficult.
zh: 这将会很困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of developers are very, very skeptic about the current state, and I think that it's going to be a lot of sales work on your end to convince, but if you're able to convince, and I think teams that are really sold on this and want to take the time to work on their workflow about how can our team become more AI native, I think that these teams are going to probably be a few years in the future and they're going to look back at the few weeks they spent setting this up as the best time they spent.
zh: 许多开发人员对当前状态非常、非常多疑，我认为你在说服方面将要做很多销售工作，但如果你能够说服，而且我认为那些真正认同这一点并愿意花时间研究其工作流程的团队，关于我们的团队如何变得更加 AI 原生化，我认为这些团队可能会领先未来几年，他们会回顾花费几周时间设置这一切的时光，认为那是他们度过的最好时光。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me ask you another question around just the job of a PM.
zh: 让我问你另一个关于 PM 工作的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the biggest fears people have with these AI tools for PMs for every function I imagine is just you start to rely on these things, your skills start to atrophy, you're producing all this slop that looks great, cool, amazing strategy doc.
zh: 人们对于 PM 的这些 AI 工具最大的恐惧之一，我想对于每个职能都是，只是你开始依赖这些东西，你的技能开始萎缩，你正在生产所有这些看起来很棒、很酷、令人惊叹的策略文档的垃圾。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, it's actually not at all good.
zh: 不，这实际上一点也不好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are these Linear tickets or just products that are half-baked?
zh: 这些是 Linear 票据还是只是半成品的产品？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's your take on these two parts of just like, how has this impacted your craft as a PM?
zh: 你对这两部分的看法是什么，就像，这如何影响了你作为 PM 的手艺？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you feel like this is weakening your skills because you're so reliant on these tools and just how do you keep the quality of this stuff up and not just like, "Eh, it's just a bunch of AI generated slop."
zh: 你是否觉得这正在削弱你的技能，因为你如此依赖这些工具，以及你如何保持这些东西的质量，而不只是像，“嗯，这只是一堆 AI 生成的垃圾。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a very strong disagree to this and I've heard it a bunch.
zh: 我对此非常强烈地不同意，我听过很多次。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember when I started using Tal Raviv has this whole course on building a PM Copilot using projects, which is probably one of the best courses that you can take.
zh: 我记得当我开始使用 Tal Raviv 拥有关于使用项目构建 PM Copilot 的完整课程时，这可能是你能参加的最好的课程之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when I started working with my own Copilot, I remember people at work looking and saying like, "Oh, so you're basically outsourcing your thinking."
zh: 当我开始与我自己的 Copilot 一起工作时，我记得工作中的同事看着并说，“哦，所以你基本上是在外包你的思考。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And to me, that's just the worst way to look at it.
zh: 对我来说，这只是看待它的最糟糕的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think for some reason, these people usually have a high correlation with the kind of person who doesn't like to show their presentation when it's only 10% done or doesn't want to ask for help a lot.
zh: 我认为出于某种原因，这些人通常与那种不喜欢在演示只完成 10% 时展示它或者不想经常寻求帮助的人有很高的相关性。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that there's a misconception with a lot of PMs that the job is always having the right answers and being the smartest person in the room.
zh: 我认为许多 PM 有一个误解，认为工作总是拥有正确的答案并成为房间里最聪明的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And at least how I was trained and how I believe the role of the PM is, it's the exact opposite.
zh: 至少在我受训的方式和我认为 PM 的角色是，这恰恰相反。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's basically harnessing anything that can get us as quick as possible to delivering the right solution to users.
zh: 它基本上是利用任何能让我们尽可能快地向用户提供正确解决方案的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just think this is like that really smart person that has context or your mentor or whatever, but is just always available and doesn't judge you and can really help you.
zh: 我只是认为这就像那个真正聪明的人，拥有背景知识或你的导师或任何东西，但总是可用并且不评判你，真的可以帮助你。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you're using it to just create your outputs and then putting them out there, yeah, that's AI slot, but it's also human error.
zh: 所以如果你使用它只是创建你的输出然后把它们发布出去，是的，那是 AI 垃圾，但这也是人为错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's really important that you own your own outputs.
zh: 我认为拥有你自己的输出非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you put anything out there or show something in a product review and you say, "Oh, sorry, that was built by AI."
zh: 如果你把任何东西发布出去或在产品评审中展示某些东西，然后你说，“哦，抱歉，那是 AI 构建的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's your mistake.
zh: 那是你的错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you use these intentionally and really take the time to understand how to use AI in the correct way, it's one of the biggest game changers that will make you much better as a PM.
zh: 我认为如果你有意地使用这些，并真正花时间理解如何以正确的方式使用 AI，这是最大的游戏规则改变者之一，会让你作为 PM 好得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And another thing here is that, especially for more junior PMs, it allows you to play at such a higher level than you would normally.
zh: 这里还有一件事是，特别是对于更初级 PM 的来说，它允许你在比通常更高的水平上发挥。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that at Wix, I wasn't thinking of what's the marketing strategy of the company and how will the onboarding be completely revamped within the whole product.
zh: 我认为在 Wix，我没有考虑公司的营销策略是什么，以及如何在整个产品中彻底改革入门引导。

speaker: Unknown
en: But on my side product, I can just do whatever decisions I want and think of the strategy and marketing and the messaging.
zh: 但在我的副业产品上，我可以做任何我想要的决定，并考虑策略、营销和传递信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is basically just getting me reps, which is one of the most important things at the beginning of your career.
zh: 这基本上只是让我获得练习，这是你职业生涯开始时最重要的事情之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I understand the fear that how do you outsource certain stuff and you're not owning 100% of everything, but I think the upside is so much more valuable.
zh: 所以我理解这种恐惧，关于你如何外包某些东西并且你没有 100% 拥有所有东西，但我认为上行空间价值要大得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the only way that AI makes you worse at your job is if you're using it wrong.
zh: 我认为 AI 唯一让你在工作中表现更差的方式就是如果你用错了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything that you've learned about reducing the sloppiness of the output, just like a tip for keeping the quality high of the stuff that it produces?
zh: 关于减少输出的草率程度，你有什么学到的吗，就是一个关于保持它产生的东西高质量的小贴士？

speaker: Unknown
en: Similar to people, setting up AI for success, for the task at hand.
zh: 就像与人一样，为 AI 设置成功，针对手头的任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if I just brought in a junior to write a deck or something and I didn't give it any guideline, I just said, "Give a strategy deck."
zh: 所以如果我只是带一个初级来写幻灯片或其他东西，我没有给它任何指导，我只是说，“给我一个策略幻灯片。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He would probably just go online and find top strategy deck and just reproduce that, which is basically what AI is doing.
zh: 他可能只是上网去找顶级策略幻灯片然后复制它，这基本上就是 AI 正在做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's basically just fed all of the internet.
zh: 它基本上只是被喂了所有的互联网。

speaker: Unknown
en: So instead of that, guiding it and giving it context on what your style of writing is and what you're trying to solve and all these different things, I think that's probably one of the biggest unlocks.
zh: 所以取而代之的是，引导它并给它关于你的写作风格是什么以及你试图解决什么以及所有这些不同事情的背景，我认为这可能是最大的解锁之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's just a quick tip.
zh: 所以这只是一个快速的小贴士。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also Cursor has a /command called deslop, which is basically going back over the code.
zh: 而且 Cursor 有一个 /command 叫 deslop，它基本上是回过头检查代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if this is integrated into the product yet, but it's on Twitter.
zh: 我不知道这是否已经集成到产品中，但它在 Twitter 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Their founders have been talking about this, so that's definitely something I would run after just to make sure that no slop is left behind.
zh: 他们的创始人一直在谈论这件事，所以我肯定会去跟进这件事，以确保没有遗留任何草率之处。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is so funny, deslop.
zh: 太搞笑了，去草率。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: One more question, which may lead to something else, but kind of going in a whole different direction.
zh: 还有一个问题，这可能会引出别的话题，但有点是往一个完全不同的方向走。

speaker: Unknown
en: You used AI to help you actually interview for the job that you got at Meta.
zh: 你用过 AI 来帮助你在 Meta 获得的那份工作的实际面试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about how you did that, because a lot of people right now are struggling to find a job reading about all these people using AI to help them interview.
zh: 聊聊你是怎么做的，因为现在很多人都在挣扎着找工作，读着关于所有这些人用 AI 帮他们面试的内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: You actually did it.
zh: 你确实做到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: What did you use?
zh: 你用了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What worked?
zh: 什么奏效了？

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like the analogy here is I have 12 nieces and nephews and you can see how people who have grown up in a different world, how their mind is formed differently.
zh: 我觉得这里的类比是，我有 12 个侄子和侄女，你可以看到在一个不同的世界里长大的人，他们的思维是如何形成不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you ask me, how do you answer a phone, I'll do this.
zh: 所以如果你问我，如何接电话，我会这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: But a child now, when you say, "How do you answer the phone?"
zh: 但是现在的孩子，当你说，「怎么接电话？」

speaker: Unknown
en: They'll do this.
zh: 他们会这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: They'll do the iPhone answer.
zh: 他们会做 iPhone 接听。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like people who are growing up now in their professional lives were the same just with AI.
zh: 我觉得现在在职场生涯中长大的人也是一样的，只是对于 AI 而言。

speaker: Unknown
en: So every time I'm faced with a new challenge or problem, I think AI first how to solve it.
zh: 所以每次我面临一个新的挑战或问题时，我会首先想如何用 AI 解决它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Meta reached out and said they'd like me to interview.
zh: 所以 Meta 联系我说他们希望我面试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Straight away, I opened up a project within Claude.
zh: 我立刻在 Claude 里创建了一个项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: I started looking online for all the best information out there, things that I resonated with.
zh: 我开始在网上寻找所有最好的信息，那些我产生共鸣的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I took a ton of frameworks and stuff from Ben Erez who has written a guest post for you, who I think is one of the best minds out there right now.
zh: 我从 Ben Erez 那里拿了一大堆框架和东西，他曾为你写过客座文章，我认为他是目前最优秀的人才之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And basically I created a project which was my coach, which I would come and consult what to do at each phase.
zh: 基本上我创建了一个项目作为我的教练，我会在每个阶段来咨询该做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would mock interview with, and this was amazing.
zh: 我会和它进行模拟面试，这太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, I created a game in Base44, which helped me ... I was really struggling with segmentation within the product questions, so thinking of the correct segments.
zh: 另外，我在 Base44 里创建了一个游戏，这帮助了我……我真的在产品问题内的细分分类上很挣扎，也就是思考正确的细分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I basically just created a quiz game, which creates questions and different segmentations and I have to choose.
zh: 所以我基本上创建了一个问答游戏，它会生成问题和不同的细分分类，而我必须做出选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this was like, I spun this up.
zh: 所以这就像，我把它搭建起来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a web app that I would play sometimes when I was on the bus to work.
zh: 这是一个 Web 应用，有时我在坐公交车去上班的时候会玩。

speaker: Unknown
en: So basically, I think Ben talks about this a bunch, so I don't just go read Ben's stuff, but just creating a project and feeding it with all the best information on the internet and then mocking a bunch.
zh: 所以基本上，我认为 Ben 谈了很多这个，所以我不仅仅是去读 Ben 的东西，而是创建一个项目并用互联网上所有最好的信息喂养它，然后进行大量的模拟。

speaker: Unknown
en: I will say that the biggest game changer for me was doing human mocks.
zh: 我要说对我来说最大的颠覆性变化是进行人类模拟。

speaker: Unknown
en: So cold outreaching to people on LinkedIn and having them do actual mocks for me, I think that at the end of the day, especially for the Meta PM prep, which is super competitive and difficult, I think there's no way to get around that.
zh: 所以在 LinkedIn 上冷联系人们，让他们为我做实际的模拟，我认为归根结底，特别是对于 Meta PM 准备，这是超级竞争和困难的，我认为这是无法绕过的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is so cool they use that post.
zh: 太酷了他们用了那篇文章。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wasn't aware.
zh: 我没意识到。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to link to it.
zh: 我们会链接到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in that post, Ben shares all these prompts you can feed ChatGPT to help you prepare for interviews, do mocks online.
zh: 在那篇文章里，Ben 分享了所有这些提示词，你可以把它们投给 ChatGPT 来帮你准备面试，在线做模拟。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a really important point to say that those take you to a point, but it's actually better to use humans.
zh: 很重要的一点要说，这些能把你带到一定阶段，但实际上使用人类更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I actually have a post coming out soon in collaboration with Noam Segal about how everyone is using AI to interview.
zh: 实际上我很快会有一篇文章发布，是与 Noam Segal 合作的，关于每个人是如何用 AI 来面试的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of the most interesting ways I've heard people and that we've found in this research was that people use it to get feedback.
zh: 我听过的最有趣的方式之一，也是我们在这项研究中发现的，是人们用它来获取反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: They record the interview and then it gives them feedback.
zh: 他们录制面试，然后它会给他们反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's where you could have done better.
zh: 这里你可以做得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's what you missed because the feedback loop is so missing.
zh: 这里是你错过的，因为反馈循环太缺失了。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one ever tells you, here's what you did badly in this interview.
zh: 没有人会告诉你，这是你这次面试做得糟糕的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one tells you that, and AI can do that.
zh: 没人告诉你那个，而 AI 可以做那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'll add two things to that.
zh: 所以我会补充两点。

speaker: Unknown
en: One, which is exactly this.
zh: 第一，正是这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'll mock with AI.
zh: 所以我会用 AI 模拟。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, I did something really cool where there's a question bank online free by Louis Lynn, which basically is an always updating bank of questions that people are asked in real interviews.
zh: 另外，我做了一件事真的很酷，网上有一个免费的题库是 Louis Lynn 做的，基本上是一个持续更新的题库，包含人们在真实面试中被问到的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I basically used Comet, which is the Perplexity's browser.
zh: 而且我基本上用了 Comet，它是 Perplexity 的浏览器。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I had the agent run all kinds of analyses on what the most asked questions are.
zh: 我让智能体对被问到最多的问题进行各种分析。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's how I knew how to prioritize what questions I would mock.
zh: 这就是我知道如何对我的模拟问题进行优先排序的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then at the end of these mocks, I would tell Claude within the project, "You're my coach and I don't want you to make me feel good.
zh: 然后在这些模拟结束时，我会告诉项目里的 Claude，「你是我的教练，我不希望你让我感觉良好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want you to make me as ready as possible for these interviews.
zh: 我希望你让我为这些面试尽可能做好准备。

speaker: Unknown
en: So give me feedback, like you said."
zh: 所以给我反馈，就像你说的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the other thing that I did was really cool was some questions where I didn't have time to mock.
zh: 我做的另一件事真的很酷，就是对于一些我没时间模拟的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would ask Claude to play the candidate, and then it would just give me a really good answer.
zh: 我会让 Claude 扮演候选人，然后它会给我一个非常好的答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I could also learn from that, like learning from someone who does a perfect answer.
zh: 我也可以从中学习，就像从一个能给出完美答案的人那里学习一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really love the way you phrased it, that people kind of in your generation, the default is, "I have something I need to do.
zh: 我真的很喜欢你的措辞方式，就是你这一代人，默认是，“我有事情要做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's go to AI immediately and help me prepare for this thing, help me figure it out."
zh: 立即去 AI 那里帮我准备这件事，帮我弄清楚。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
